title: salix lapponum subsp. helvetica
intro: Salix lapponum subsp. helvetica is a subspecies of willow tree that is native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its slender branches and heart-shaped leaves. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Salix lapponum subsp. helvetica include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the bark and leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
